channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rubio, Tim Scott would give Trump biggest boost as vice president: Poll
item.description: Sens. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) and Tim Scott (R-S.C.) are the two vice-presidential contenders who would offer the biggest boost to former President Trump if they were selected as his running mate, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's campaign announces a $264 million fundraising haul in 2nd quarter during post-debate anxiety
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden 's reelection campaign and the Democratic National Committee reported raising $264 million in the year's second quarter, an impressive haul that may help them calm fears within their own party about last week's shaky debate performance.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cup Noodles releases new s'mores flavor for summer
item.description: Nissin Foods USA announced they're releasing a new limited-edition Cup Noodles Campfire S'mores flavor of ramen noodles.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, KRON4

item.title: Hungary's leader is in Ukraine. It's the first visit by Russia’s top EU ally since the war began
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was in Kyiv on Tuesday for talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, his first visit to the neighboring country since Russia’s full-scale invasion in February 2022 as Budapest has repeatedly broken ranks with the rest of the Eu
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pål Enger, Norwegian art thief behind famed 1994 heist of Munch's 'The Scream,' dead at 57
item.description: HELSINKI (AP) — Pål Enger, a talented Norwegian soccer player turned celebrity art thief who pulled off the sensational 1994 heist of Edvard Munch’s famed “The Scream” painting from the National Gallery in Oslo, has died. He was 57.
item.dc:creator: JARI TANNER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mars and meteors: July skygazing guide
item.description: July promises to be a celestial feast for skygazers, with events ranging from meteor showers to planetary alignments.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KRON4

item.title: Texas hiker dies on trail at Grand Canyon National Park; officials warn of extreme heat
item.description: Scott Sims, a 69-year-old man from Austin, Texas, died hiking the River Trail while trying to reach Phantom Ranch at Grand Canyon National Park.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France's far-right National Rally says it will lead a government only with an absolute majority
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The star president of France's National Rally will take the helm of government only if the party wins an absolute majority in Sunday's second round of legislative elections, the leading far-right figure Marine Le Pen said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden administration proposes rule for workplaces to address excessive heat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration proposed a new rule Tuesday to address excessive heat in the workplace, as tens of millions of people in the U.S. are under heat advisories due to blistering temperatures. If finalized, the measure would protect an estimated 36 million U.S.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How the Supreme Court presidential immunity ruling impacts the Biden-Trump rematch and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the hot water fueling Hurricane Beryl foretells a scary storm season
item.description: Hurricane Beryl's explosive growth into an unprecedented early whopper of a storm shows the literal hot water the Atlantic and Caribbean are in right now and the kind of season ahead, experts said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What we know about the fatal police shooting of a 13-year-old boy in upstate New York
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Police in upstate New York tackled and then shot a 13-year-old boy to death after he pointed what turned out to be a BB gun at officers during a foot chase.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden proposes first-ever nationwide worker protections from heat
item.description: As the nation sizzles, the Biden administration is proposing the nation’s first-ever standards aimed at protecting workers from extreme heat.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee woman awarded nearly $700k after being fired for refusing COVID-19 vaccine requirement
item.description: A Tennessee woman who was fired for refusing BlueCross BlueShield of Tennessee's COVID-19 employee vaccine mandate received a nearly $700,000 settlement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Darrell Christian, former AP managing editor and sports editor, dies at 75
item.description: Darrell L. Christian, a former managing editor and sports editor of The Associated Press known for a demanding demeanor and insistence on excellence during more than four decades with the news agency, died Monday. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Final person to plead guilty in Denver fire that killed 5 people from Senegal could get 60 years
item.description: DENVER (AP) — In between shifts at Amazon to earn money she could send home to her relatives in Senegal — working opposite times as her sister-in-law so they could care for each other's children — Hassan Diol would call to talk to her husband several times a day.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Karen Read murder case ends in a mistrial. Prosecutors say they will try again
item.description: DEDHAM, Mass. (AP) — A judge declared a mistrial Monday after jurors deadlocked in the polarizing and much-watched case of Karen Read, a woman accused of striking her Boston police officer boyfriend with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Things to know about the Karen Read murder case, which has ended in a mistrial
item.description: DEDHAM, Mass. (AP) — A judge declared a mistrial Monday after jurors deadlocked in the case of Karen Read, a woman accused of killing her Boston police officer boyfriend by striking him with her SUV and leaving him in a snowstorm.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who confessed to killing 4 people in Maine, including his parents, sentenced to life in prison
item.description: WEST BATH, Maine (AP) — A man who confessed to killing his parents and two of their friends and wounding three people in a highway shooting pleaded guilty to murder and other charges on Monday, and a judge sentenced him to the maximum term of life in prison.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mars and meteors: July skygazing guide
item.description: July promises to be a celestial feast for skygazers, with events ranging from meteor showers to planetary alignments.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump seeks to set aside hush money verdict hours after immunity ruling
item.description: In prior court filings, Trump contended he is immune from prosecution for conduct alleged to involve official acts during his tenure in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump seeks to set aside New York verdict hours after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Donald Trump's lawyers on Monday asked the New York judge who presided over his hush money trial to set aside his conviction and delay his sentencing scheduled for later this month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: All Republican women in South Carolina Senate voted out of office
item.description: The only three Republican women in the South Carolina Senate took on their party and stopped a total abortion ban from passing in their state last year. In return, they lost their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hunter Biden sues Fox News over miniseries
item.description: Hunter Biden has sued Fox News over a miniseries it published and then took down from its subscriber streaming service Fox Nation.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Trump golf courses' liquor licenses not renewed after conviction
item.description: The licenses expired Sunday, but the two golf clubs were issued interim permits to serve alcohol until a hearing is held on the matter. The hearing is scheduled for July 19, which the division noted is after Trump’s sentencing on July 11.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sforza, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have some immunity, sends Trump case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Who is Gavin Newsom? Top Democrat floated as Biden replacement
item.description: After Biden's shaky debate performance, Gov. Gavin Newsom is one of the top names for his possible replacement.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: BET apologizes to Usher for 'audio malfunction' during awards acceptance speech
item.description: Usher received a major honor during Sunday night's BET Awards at the Peacock Theater in Downtown Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Costco CEO says this is the most important item the store sells
item.description: Costco warehouses seemingly have everything from the popular $1.50 hot dog combo and affordable gasoline to gold bars and electronics.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A media 'nervous breakdown'? Calls for Biden's withdrawal produce some extraordinary moments
item.description: President Biden's performance in last week's debate with Donald Trump led to some extraordinary media moments.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Stingray that got pregnant despite no male companion has died, aquarium says
item.description: Video above: New encounter at ZooTampa allows you to get into the water with stingrays HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — A stingray that got pregnant at a North Carolina aquarium this winter despite not having shared a tank with a male of her species for many years has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How the hot water that fueled Hurricane Beryl foretells a scary storm season
item.description: Hurricane Beryl’s explosive growth into an unprecedented early storm shows the literal hot water the Atlantic and Caribbean are in right now and the kind of season ahead.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 20 riders rescued from Disney's Incredicoaster
item.description: Twenty people had to be escorted off the Disney California Adventure Park’s Incredicoaster after the ride stopped on the tracks Sunday. The stoppage occurred around 1:30 p.m. at the park located in Anaheim.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump lead over Biden unchanged since debate: Poll
item.description: Former President Trump’s lead over President Biden in national polling is unchanged following the debate between the candidates last week, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for a North Dakota truck stop in a new blow to federal regulators
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court opened the door Monday to new, broad challenges to regulations long after they take effect, the third blow in a week to federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL QB, JaMarcus Russell, sued over $74k donation intended for alma mater
item.description: Former NFL No. 1 overall draft pick and LSU standout quarterback JaMarcus Russell is facing a lawsuit over a $74,000 check written as a donation for the football program at his alma mater, Williamson High School.
item.dc:creator: Simone Eli, Fox 5

item.title: Patelco Credit Union 'serious security incident' leaves customers unable to bank online
item.description: Patelco Credit Union customers in Northern California were still waiting for answers Monday after the bank announced over the weekend that its systems experienced a serious security incident.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, Fox 5
